title: agropyron truncatum subsp. trichophorum
intro: Agropyron truncatum subsp. trichophorum, also known as False Wheat Grass or Wild Oats, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues and reducing stress, and it has been traditionally used to treat conditions like fever and respiratory problems. Agropyron truncatum subsp. trichophorum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal medicine in the form of dried roots and leaves.
